original_image_tags: * Top-left black-and-white photograph of a grainy sky with a faint, circular object circled in dark ink.
* Bottom-left black-and-white photograph of a grainy sky with a faint, circular object circled in dark ink.
* Right-side diagram showing a cross-section of a disc-shaped object. The diagram is drawn in white lines on a dark background. It shows a central hull and four surrounding planes labeled:

elements.description: A Spanish-language newspaper clipping from 'MEXICO, D. F., JUEVES 16 DE MARZO DE 1950' with the headline 'Las Primeras Fotografías de un "Plato Volador", Logradas en Durango a Nueve mil Pies de Altura'. It contains three distinct visual elements: 1) A photograph of a circular object in a grainy sky. 2) A photograph of a similar circular object in a grainy sky. 3) A hand-drawn sketch of a diamond or eye-shaped object with labeled sections ('1er Plano', '2o Plano', '3er Plano', etc.) and arrows indicating airflow or movement. A caption at the bottom begins 'El señor German Horacio Robles, Jr., pasante de la Escuela Nacional de Ingeniería...'.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on three visual elements: two photographs of a circular object in a grainy sky and one diagram/sketch, with Description B adding specific caption details omitted by Description A without contradiction.
